incentive.statement: A pending causative or applicative formation must not erase source-authorized Mood and Tense choices from Authority #2.

coordinator.responsibility: Coordinate the capability, UI, and verification repair without weakening canonical derivation selection.

outcome: Direct, pending Causative, and pending Applicative expose the same source-authorized Mood and Tense decisions while derivation formation and downstream choices remain typed and gated.

responsibilities.statement: Derive pending-result UI capabilities from the canonical source machinery without authorizing the unresolved derivation.

actors.contribution: Preserve the source machinery kind as the capability basis during a typed derivation-selection block.

responsibilities.statement: Render source-authorized Mood and Tense as enabled while leaving genuine formation, voice, and participant gates intact.

actors.contribution: Consume the capability frame without adding renderer-owned grammar logic.

responsibilities.statement: Prove pending derivation blocks coexist with selectable Mood and Tense in all three derivation modes.

actors.contribution: Add focused hostile and live-browser checks for capability parity and retained derivation blocking.

responsibilities.statement: Record the bounded target, work-sizing rule, and focused evidence without starting the full readiness gate.

actors.contribution: Keep the implementation and verification boundary explicit.

retirements.removalEvidence: Pending Causative and Applicative capability frames retain canonical source Mood and Tense availability.

evidence.result: UI and causative relation UI passed 392/392 assertions; classical VNC application passed 25/25.
Before generated-formation selection, Causative accepted optative plus nonpast and Applicative kept Mood and Tense enabled; both formation controls remained required and blank.

closure.nextLessonEntryPoint: Select the next explicit implementation target; full readiness belongs to the formal handoff.
